vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on Backup gshadow File
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /etc/gshadow-, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0000 /etc/gshadow-

triage_decision.reason: Changing the permissions of the backup gshadow file is a standard STIG hardening step that does not affect active authentication mechanisms or system boot. The remediation is a simple chmod command, posing minimal risk of lockout or service disruption.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; filesystems affected; If permissions remain too permissive, backup shadow data could be exposed
